topStories.description: Unverified photos of the jet showed its tail completely severed, rendering it unflyable.

topStories.title: Incarceration without trial is punishment, says Supreme Court; grants bail to accused in jail for two years
topStories.description: A bench of Justices Dipankar Datta and P.V. Varale, in its recent order, said Pradeep Kumar alias Banu was booked for various offences, including attempt to murder, in February 2024, but the prosecution is yet to examine any one of the 23 witnesses related to the case

topStories.title: Amit Shah says curbing ‘infiltration not enough’, every illegal immigrant must be deported from Assam
topStories.description: Claiming that "Assam State Congress president Gaurav Gogoi wants to protect infiltrators", Amit Shah asked him to clarify his stand on the issue.

topStories.title: US Stocks Markets | Lucrative bets that anticipated Trump's policy surprises warrant scrutiny, experts say
topStories.description: Well-timed trades ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's major policy surprises ​during his second term have potentially led to millions of dollars in profits for unknown ​traders, leading some legal experts to say they should be investigated to protect fair markets and ascertain whether information is leaking.

dailyNews.title: Pakistan hosts top Saudi, Turkish, Egyptian diplomats over war in Iran
dailyNews.description: Talks are under way in Islamabad, as the Pakistani government acts as mediator between the US and Iran.

dailyNews.title: America Is Depleting a More Powerful Weapon Than Its Missiles
dailyNews.description: It was reported last week that the Iranian missile inventory has been depleted from about 5,000 to a thousand or so, and that the US and its allies are now firing one or two Patriots at each incoming airborne threat, in place of the clusters unleashed at the start. In other words, both sides are experiencing munitions shortages.

dailyNews.title: Iran’s IRGC claims attacks on UAE, Bahrain aluminium facilities
dailyNews.description: Iran targets Aluminium Bahrain, Emirates Global Aluminium in retaliation for US-Israeli attacks on its infrastructure.

dailyNews.title: ‘Trump has to open the Strait of Hormuz’ with ground forces
dailyNews.description: Kenneth Katzman says US war objectives in Iran that cannot be met with air power can be met with a ground invasion.

dailyNews.title: Toddler rescued after US-Israeli strike on Iran
dailyNews.description: Rescue teams from the Iranian Red Crescent rescued a toddler from a destroyed building after a US-Israeli strike.

dailyNews.title: How can Houthis threaten US interests? Why Bab el-Mandeb Strait matters and what happens if it's blocked?
dailyNews.description: Closing the Bab el-Mandeb Strait would hinder oil tanker access to the Suez Canal, disrupting global energy routes.

dailyNews.title: Belgium thrash USA 5-2; Mexico vs Portugal ends 0-0 in football friendlies
dailyNews.description: A man died after falling from the stands at Azteca Stadium, where Mexico hosted Portugal with Ronaldo missing the match.

dailyNews.title: Catholic conversions rise as young adults 'hungry for truth' turn to faith and reject secularism, bishop says
dailyNews.description: Catholic dioceses across the U.S. are reporting record adult converts, with Bishop Robert Barron saying young adults are leading a return to faith.

dailyNews.title: Cases of 'white plague' rising in US as doctors warn of 'rebound effect'
dailyNews.description: Tuberculosis, also known as "the white plague," is rising in the U.S. post-pandemic, with over 10,600 cases confirmed in 2024, the CDC reports.

dailyNews.title: DHS shutdown putting Americans at risk as World Cup security prep ‘significantly behind’: Sen Fetterman
dailyNews.description: Sen. John Fetterman urges an end to the weeks-long DHS shutdown, warning that TSA staffing losses threaten airport security ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

dailyNews.title: African nation calls for Ilhan Omar to be extradited after Vance's fraud claim
dailyNews.description: The Republic of Somaliland has called for Rep. Ilhan Omar's extradition after Vice President JD Vance claimed she committed immigration fraud, an accusation Omar has denied.

topStories.description: The war in Iran was a galvanizing force, but plenty of protesters focused on President Trump’s immigration crackdown. Senate candidates in several key races joined the crowds.

topStories.title: ‘No Kings’ Protests Decry Trump and His Agenda

topStories.title: No Kings Protests Held Across the U.S.: Photos and Videos
topStories.description: It’s the third time that the coalition behind the “No Kings” movement has organized events to protest President Trump and his policies. In the United States, more than 3,000 demonstrations were planned.

dailyNews.description: Protesters across the U.S. and the world took to the streets as part of the latest "No Kings" rallies against President Trump and his administration. Among the biggest was in St. Paul, Minnesota. Cristian Benavides reports.

dailyNews.title: How the big oil and gas CEOs think the Iran war supply disruption will play out
dailyNews.description: The oil industry's grim outlook for global oil and gas supplies stands in contrast to the Trump administration's promise that the disruption is short term.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: IDF launches 'widespread' strikes in Tehran
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israeli strikes.

dailyNews.title: Trump fans at CPAC hope for the best in Iran but worry over the cost at home
dailyNews.description: The U.S. war with Iran hung over CPAC, the annual conservative conference, where attendees said they trust Trump while hoping the conflict ends soon.

dailyNews.title: Left in limbo, Afghans who served with the U.S. fear Trump could send them back to the Taliban
dailyNews.description: On a former U.S. military base in Qatar, Afghans who supported the United States in its 20-year war against the Taliban have been left in limbo, living in windowless shipping containers far from the new lives they were once promised in the U.S.Now, the Trump administration is presenting them with a stark choice: move to an unspecified third country or return to Taliban-ruled Afghanistan, where they potentially face persecution, imprisonment or death

dailyNews.title: Pentagon prepares for weeks of ground operations in Iran
dailyNews.description: If President Trump approves the plans, such an effort would mark a new phase of the war that could be significantly more dangerous to U.S. troops than the first four weeks.

dailyNews.title: MAGA’s eruption has so far kept Trump from endorsing in key Senate race
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump seemed set to endorse Sen. John Cornyn in the Texas Republican primary runoff. But strong opposition from MAGA activists forestalled that.

dailyNews.title: Trump ratchets up attacks on NATO, says U.S. no longer needs alliance
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump suggested the U.S. might move away from its commitment to defend NATO allies, criticizing them for not supporting the war in Iran.
